TASK-001.title: Lock product intent in specs
TASK-001.summary: Turn codewiki intent into explicit product and system docs.

goal.outcome: Project intent and ownership boundaries are explicit enough to guide implementation.
acceptance: Foundational specs describe desired outcomes and major constraints.
At least one roadmap task links back to those specs.
non_goals: Document every implementation detail before the project has real seams.
verification: Review starter specs for project-specific intent and ownership coverage.
Run the rebuild command after replacing placeholders.

evidence.summary: Foundational product and system specs now describe CodeWiki intent, users, success criteria, non-goals, ownership seams, runtime boundary, evidence model, planning/task loops, and architecture review rule. Roadmap tasks link back to these specs.

TASK-002.title: Map code ownership into spec hierarchy
TASK-002.summary: Refine the inferred boundary docs until wiki/system mirrors the repo's real ownership seams without creating doc sprawl.

goal.outcome: wiki/system reflects real stable ownership seams in the repo.
acceptance: Each meaningful code area maps to one canonical owning spec.
Unnecessary inferred boundaries are removed or collapsed.
non_goals: Create a spec for every folder regardless of architectural value.
verification: Review inferred boundary docs against actual repo structure.
Run rebuild and inspect mapping/drift output.

evidence.summary: Mapped real package ownership seams into system specs: extension/package surface owns commands, tools, status UI, packaged skills, bootstrap templates, smoke tests, and root discovery; runtime policy owns transaction/verifier/capability boundary.

TASK-003.title: Keep roadmap as freshest delta log
TASK-003.summary: Move drift and plan tracking into structured roadmap tasks instead of separate prose buckets.

goal.outcome: Tracked delta lives in roadmap tasks instead of scattered prose.
acceptance: Active implementation gaps are represented by roadmap tasks.
Users can resume task work from Pi surfaces without editing roadmap JSON manually.
non_goals: Maintain separate plan and drift documents for the same live work.
verification: Inspect generated roadmap view after rebuild.
Confirm roadmap tasks cover current active delta.

evidence.summary: Roadmap surface and client overview now define roadmap tasks as the freshest delta log for planning notes, drift findings, architecture candidates, verification failures, and next actions; active brainstormed work has been converted into structured TASK records.

TASK-004.title: Expose CodeWiki capability manifest and think-code boundary
TASK-004.summary: Define the typed CodeWiki capabilities that Pi runtimes such as think-code can call without bypassing .wiki semantics, including optional executor/fallback boundaries.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki exposes a clear capability/transaction surface for reads, task mutations, evidence appends, graph rebuilds, and validated wiki edits while composing with think-code as an optional bounded executor.
acceptance: Runtime policy spec names the capability classes CodeWiki owns.
Manifest shape includes capability name, args schema, result schema, read/write classification, and audit fields.
Raw writes to generated state remain out of scope for external runtimes.
Plan covers behavior when think-code is not installed.
Plan covers project .wiki read access and write delegation rules.
non_goals: Implement a general sandbox.
Duplicate telemetry or eval extension behavior.
Make think-code a hard dependency before the integration contract is stable.
verification: Review against think-code capability boundary task
Run codewiki state refresh after spec changes
Compare intended behavior in Pi sessions with and without think-code installed

evidence.summary: Implemented CodeWiki capability manifest in gateway and runtime spec. Manifest names semantic capability classes for state, task mutation, session notes, validated transactions, and derived rebuilds with args/result schema names, read/write classification, affected writes, and audit fields. Spec documents think-code fallback and generated-state write delegation rules.
checks_run: node scripts/codewiki-gateway.mjs manifest | node -e JSON.parse validation

TASK-005.title: Split CodeWiki skills into focused outer and inner workflows
TASK-005.summary: Replace the single monolithic CodeWiki skill with a package-level router plus focused CodeWiki skills for planning, task execution, research, and verification.

goal.outcome: Agents load only the CodeWiki workflow instructions needed for the current job while preserving shared CodeWiki invariants.
acceptance: A small `codewiki` router skill states package invariants and when to load each focused skill.
Focused skills exist for `codewiki-plan`, `codewiki-task`, `codewiki-research`, and `codewiki-verify`.
Skill descriptions are precise enough for Pi progressive disclosure and do not duplicate large workflow text.
All focused skills preserve `.wiki/knowledge` as canonical intent and roadmap/evidence as machine-managed operational truth.
non_goals: Install unrelated global skills as part of this repo task.
Replace CodeWiki tools or roadmap schema.
verification: Run package typecheck/tests after skill/package changes.
Use Pi startup or package listing to confirm focused skills are discoverable.
Review each skill for overlap and conflicting instructions.

evidence.summary: Re-verified TASK-005 after correcting stale spec path; focused skill split remains implemented and verified.

TASK-006.title: Define CodeWiki outer planning loop
TASK-006.summary: Specify the automatic outer loop that turns user intent into wiki knowledge updates and roadmap tasks before task execution begins, with user approval only at decision checkpoints.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki has an automatic planning workflow for clarifying intent, gathering evidence when needed, updating knowledge, and creating roadmap tasks.
acceptance: Planning loop is documented as clarify/inspect/research-if-needed/update-knowledge/create-roadmap-tasks with automatic progression where safe.
Research is explicitly outside normal task execution and supports knowledge claims.
Roadmap tasks are created only after intended behavior and acceptance criteria are clear enough.
User-facing commands/prompts can start the outer loop, but the user does not manually trigger each stage.
The loop pauses for user input only when ambiguity, product choice, destructive change, or approval policy requires it.
non_goals: Implement every planned task.
Turn CodeWiki roadmap into an external issue tracker.
Remove human approval from material product or architecture decisions.
verification: Review generated roadmap tasks for spec links, outcomes, acceptance, non-goals, and verification.
Run codewiki state refresh and confirm roadmap summary remains healthy.
Dry-run a planning request and confirm stages advance without manual command choreography.

evidence.summary: Verified automatic outer planning loop implementation in focused skill and client roadmap specs after final checks.

TASK-007.title: Define CodeWiki task execution inner loop
TASK-007.summary: Specify the automatic per-task loop that uses think-code context packets, implementation, local checks, fresh verification, and evidence before closure without requiring user-triggered verification commands.

goal.outcome: Each roadmap task has an automatic implement→verify loop grounded in task context, tests, and evidence.
acceptance: Task loop is documented as load task/context, create compact context with think-code when useful, implement, run local checks, automatically invoke fresh verification when ready, append evidence, then close/block/follow-up.
Local verification and fresh-context verification have distinct responsibilities.
Task evidence captures checks run, files touched, issues, and verifier verdict.
Closure criteria require acceptance criteria and verification evidence, not just model confidence.
User command/manual trigger is treated as override/debug, not normal flow.
non_goals: Force TDD for every trivial documentation-only task.
Make think-code a hard dependency when unavailable.
verification: Run a sample task through the documented automatic loop or dry-run the workflow with an existing task.
Confirm task history/evidence records contain enough data to resume or audit work.
Confirm task closure cannot skip required verifier stage unless policy explicitly allows it.

evidence.summary: Verified automatic inner task loop implementation after final checks.

TASK-008.title: Model research evidence as support for wiki knowledge
TASK-008.summary: Separate external/source research from task execution so research evidence supports `.wiki/knowledge` claims rather than becoming part of every implementation loop.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki distinguishes research evidence for intended knowledge from execution evidence for task closure.
acceptance: Research workflow records cited findings as support for knowledge updates and design decisions.
Task execution loop treats research as optional only when uncertainty blocks implementation or claims need support.
Evidence schema or conventions distinguish source/research evidence from checks/execution evidence.
The research skill explains when to use web/code research versus local code inspection.
non_goals: Require web research for routine code changes.
Create a separate ADR system outside `.wiki/knowledge`.
verification: Review evidence entries produced by sample research and sample task execution for clear classification.
Run codewiki state refresh and confirm no generated state is hand-edited.

evidence.summary: Verified research evidence model implementation after final checks.

TASK-009.title: Add automatic fresh-context verifier agent workflow
TASK-009.summary: Design and implement an automatic verifier stage that spawns a read-only agent when task execution reaches verification, while retaining a manual command only as an override/debug entrypoint.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki automatically verifies task completion through a separate read-only Pi agent as part of the inner loop before closure.
acceptance: Inner loop invokes verifier automatically when a task is ready for closure or explicit verification evidence is missing.
Verifier stage accepts a task id internally and builds a compact verification brief from task context, linked specs, diff/status, and acceptance criteria.
Verifier runs in a separate Pi process or SDK/RPC session with read-only tools plus allowed check commands.
Verifier returns deterministic JSON verdict with pass/fail/block, checks, issues, and rationale.
Parent CodeWiki flow appends verifier output as task evidence without automatically mutating code.
Auto-close is gated by verifier pass plus local checks plus no unresolved issues and user/project policy.
Manual `/wiki-verify` or equivalent, if present, is a debug/override entrypoint rather than the primary workflow.
non_goals: Give verifier write/edit/apply authority.
Replace human review for security-sensitive or product-sensitive changes.
Require verifier for every tiny docs typo when policy says skip.
verification: Create tests or smoke scripts for verifier trigger selection, brief generation, and verdict parsing.
Manual smoke: run automatic verifier stage against a known passing and known failing task fixture.
Confirm verifier cannot write files or mutate roadmap directly.

evidence.summary: Implemented automatic fresh verifier stage for explicit task close: builds compact verifier prompt from task/context, spawns read-only `pi --mode json --no-session` with read/grep/find/ls tools, parses deterministic JSON verdict, appends verifier evidence, and blocks closure on non-pass; smoke tests disable nested verifier via env.
issues: Automatic verifier currently runs on explicit `codewiki_task` close; lifecycle `update` pass path remains a follow-up if strict auto-verify is required there too.

TASK-010.title: Add CodeWiki architecture review workflow adapted from deep-module heuristics
TASK-010.summary: Adapt deep-module and shared-language architecture review ideas into a `.wiki`-aware workflow for finding seams, locality problems, and roadmap-worthy refactors.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki can run architecture reviews that produce knowledge updates and roadmap tasks rather than ad hoc refactor suggestions.
acceptance: Workflow uses CodeWiki vocabulary and `.wiki/knowledge` instead of `CONTEXT.md`/ADR assumptions.
Review rubric includes module/interface/implementation, seam, adapter, locality, leverage, and deletion test.
Findings become either knowledge clarifications, roadmap tasks, or explicitly deferred non-goals.
Architecture review does not propose interfaces before presenting friction candidates and asking user to pick.
non_goals: Create a separate ADR tree outside `.wiki/knowledge`.
Automatically refactor architecture during review.
verification: Run the workflow on the CodeWiki repo and produce at least one candidate or documented no-op result.
Confirm any resulting tasks link to owning specs and acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: Implemented CodeWiki architecture review workflow with deep-module vocabulary, friction candidate process, and system spec guidance that turns findings into knowledge updates, roadmap tasks, or non-goals.

TASK-011.title: Record curated global agent skills decision
TASK-011.summary: Document which general engineering skills should be installed or adapted globally for Pi without conflicting with CodeWiki-specific workflows.

goal.outcome: There is a durable CodeWiki decision record for global Pi skills versus CodeWiki-specific skills.
acceptance: Global candidates are tdd, diagnose, grill-me, zoom-out, and write-a-skill.
Excluded or adapt-first skills are documented with reasons.
Notes include Pi package security and skill discovery behavior.
No global Pi settings are changed without explicit user approval.
non_goals: Install all external skills immediately.
Replace CodeWiki roadmap with external issue skills.
verification: Review decision text against Pi docs and existing global packages.
Confirm no repo-specific conventions are forced globally.

evidence.summary: Recorded curated global skills decision as CodeWiki knowledge, including recommended global skills, adapt-first/excluded skills, and compatibility rules with Pi packages and CodeWiki semantics.

TASK-012.title: Reduce canonical surface and projection write amplification
TASK-012.summary: Projection firewall first slice completed: canonical/generated contract documented and refresh=false paths now avoid projection locks/rebuilds where safe.

goal.outcome: Normal CodeWiki updates write only canonical truth while generated graph/status/lint/roadmap views are rebuilt as disposable projections.
acceptance: Canonical and generated file classes are documented in system knowledge.
Generated projection files are marked or described as tool-owned/read-only for agents.
At least one mutator path avoids unnecessary generated projection writes unless refresh is explicitly requested.
codewiki_state still returns current status after refresh.
Typecheck and tests pass.
non_goals: Remove all generated files from the repository.
Implement the architecture diagram projection.
Rewrite the whole roadmap storage model.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test

evidence.summary: Closed after typecheck and smoke/package tests passed.
issues: Further slices should decouple close/archive retention from rebuild before changing codewiki_task default refresh.

TASK-013.title: Decouple closed-task retention from projection rebuild
TASK-013.summary: Closed-task retention no longer depends on generated projection rebuilds; refresh=false close path preserves archive retention.

goal.outcome: Closing tasks can enforce roadmap archive retention through the canonical task mutation path without depending on rebuild side effects.
acceptance: Task close with closed_task_limit=0 archives the closed task even when projection refresh is false.
Generated projection rebuild remains available and does not become the only retention mechanism.
Smoke coverage verifies archive retention for refresh=false or equivalent minimal-write close path.
Typecheck and tests pass.
non_goals: Remove Python rebuild retention immediately.
Change archive file format.
Change default retention policy.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test

evidence.summary: Closed after typecheck, smoke, and pack tests passed.

TASK-014.title: Capture CodeWiki v2 operating model and roadmap
TASK-014.summary: CodeWiki v2 decisions are now durable wiki knowledge and mapped to executable roadmap tasks.

goal.outcome: The wiki records the v2 canonical/view model, product split, memory policy, subagent/heartbeat/think-code roles, and sanitation/versioning policy as roadmap-backed intent.
acceptance: V2 decisions are captured in canonical knowledge docs, not only chat history.
Product knowledge is split or prepared for users, stories, and surfaces.
The system knowledge names the canonical/view boundary and view consumption model.
Roadmap tasks cover v2 migration, views, architecture graph, subagents, heartbeat, think-code, and sanitation/release policy.
codewiki_state reports the new roadmap without drift.
non_goals: Implement the full v2 storage migration.
Render architecture diagrams in the status panel.
Rename all code-level projection terminology immediately.
verification: codewiki_state refresh=true

evidence.summary: Closed after codewiki_state reported no unmapped specs and v2 roadmap tasks were created.

TASK-015.title: Implement v2 wiki layout and migration
TASK-015.summary: V2 layout compatibility is implemented: `.wiki/views/**` is generated alongside legacy read models and bootstrap/rebuild understand the v2 views root.

goal.outcome: New and migrated repos use canonical knowledge/tasks/evidence plus generated views under the v2 structure without breaking legacy paths.
acceptance: V2 target paths are represented in config/templates.
Legacy `.wiki/roadmap.json` and current generated files remain readable during migration.
Migration or bootstrap creates the v2 directory shape safely.
Rebuild can populate v2 views while preserving current tool behavior.
non_goals: Remove all legacy paths in one release.
Rewrite every tool call to the final v2 paths immediately.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test

evidence.summary: Closed after typecheck, rebuild, smoke, and pack tests passed.

TASK-016.title: Bake product split and lexicon into templates
TASK-016.summary: Starter templates now include the v2 product split and shared lexicon, with smoke coverage for the new files.

goal.outcome: New wikis start with focused product knowledge and a shared lexicon rather than one large product overview.
acceptance: Bootstrap templates include lexicon, users, stories, and surfaces docs.
Product overview is short navigation plus boundaries.
Smoke tests cover expected starter files.
Existing clients docs link cleanly to the product split.
non_goals: Delete all client surface docs.
Implement system architecture graph generation.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test

evidence.summary: Closed after rebuild, typecheck, smoke, and pack tests passed.

TASK-017.title: Render architecture graph in status panel
TASK-017.summary: Update the CodeWiki status panel to render the generated architecture view and show component or flow details when selected.

goal.outcome: Humans and agents can inspect system architecture visually through the status panel and zoom into component or flow detail cards.
acceptance: Status panel renders the generated architecture graph or a fallback graph view.
Selecting a component opens linked component markdown/details.
Selecting a flow opens linked flow markdown/details.
Health colors use strict legend semantics from roadmap/drift/view state.
Panel degrades gracefully when architecture views are stale or absent.
non_goals: Make the panel a full diagram editor.
Hand-edit Mermaid in the UI.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test

evidence.summary: Implemented architecture fallback display and richer wiki detail cards; ready for verification.
issues: Generated architecture JSON/Mermaid remains TASK-020; panel degrades to system-doc fallback until that view exists.

TASK-018.title: Implement context memory policy in skills and loops
TASK-018.summary: Teach agents when to use session RAM, views, canonical docs, subagents, and ThinkCode so default workflows minimize repeated token load.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki skills and loops default to tiny views and targeted expansion instead of reloading raw wiki state into the parent session.
acceptance: Planning, task, verification, research, and architecture skills describe RAM vs persistent memory rules.
Default flow starts from status view and recommended next reads.
Agents track loaded view revisions and avoid rereading unchanged views when possible.
Task and outer-loop instructions explain when to spawn subagents or ThinkCode.
non_goals: Implement full heartbeat scheduler.
Build new view schemas from scratch if TASK-018 is not done.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test

evidence.summary: Updated memory policy across skills and prompts; ready for closure after passing checks.

TASK-019.title: View schema generator for status queue drift and briefs
TASK-019.summary: Define and generate the small, revisioned read models agents and UI consume for status, roadmap queue, task context, drift, product brief, system architecture, and recent evidence.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki exposes compact generated views that route agents to the right next read without loading raw wiki state.
acceptance: Views include revision metadata, stale flags where applicable, size budgets, and recommended next reads.
Status, roadmap queue, task context, drift, product brief, system architecture, and recent evidence views are defined.
`codewiki_state` can expose or consume the views without increasing default token load.
Views remain rebuild-owned and read-only to agents.
non_goals: Build status panel graph interaction.
Implement heartbeat autonomy.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test
node scripts/rebuild_docs_meta.py

evidence.summary: Generated v2 views for status, roadmap queue, drift, product brief, system architecture, recent evidence, and mirrored task contexts with revision metadata, budgets, and recommended next reads.

TASK-020.title: Architecture manifest and Mermaid view generator
TASK-020.summary: Add canonical system architecture graph support with component and flow docs, then generate agent-readable and Mermaid architecture views from it.

goal.outcome: System architecture is represented as a structured canonical graph with component and flow detail docs and generated visual/read views.
acceptance: `system/architecture.json` owns component nodes and information-flow edges.
`system/components/*.md` detail component cards.
`system/flows/*.md` detail important cross-component paths.
Rebuild generates a Mermaid architecture view and agent-readable system architecture view.
Graph validation detects missing component/flow docs or orphan docs.
non_goals: Implement status panel click interactions in this task.
Make Mermaid the source of truth.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test
node scripts/rebuild_docs_meta.py

evidence.summary: Added canonical system architecture manifest, component/flow docs, architecture view generation, Mermaid rendering, validation for missing/orphan component and flow docs, and bootstrap smoke coverage.

TASK-021.title: Subagent JSON worker contracts
TASK-021.summary: Define and implement compact JSON contracts for verifier, researcher, planner, architecture reviewer, and view auditor subagents.

goal.outcome: Context-heavy or bias-sensitive CodeWiki work can run in fresh subagent sessions and return bounded results to the parent agent.
acceptance: Verifier, researcher, planner, architecture reviewer, and view auditor roles have documented input/output contracts.
Parent agent remains responsible for canonical writes.
Subagent outputs are compact JSON with pass/fail/block or proposal semantics as appropriate.
Existing fresh verifier behavior aligns with the shared subagent model.
non_goals: Let subagents freely mutate `.wiki` canonical truth.
Replace all parent-agent work with subagents.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test

evidence.summary: Added shared SubagentBrief/SubagentResult contracts plus role-specific contracts for verifier, researcher, planner, architecture reviewer, and view auditor. Added view-audit skill and smoke coverage for package skill loading.

TASK-022.title: Heartbeat modes budgets and stop conditions
TASK-022.summary: Add bounded observe, maintain, and work heartbeat modes that use views, subagents, and canonical writes safely.

goal.outcome: Users can run CodeWiki autonomously within explicit budgets and modes without losing alignment with wiki truth or project safety.
acceptance: Heartbeat supports observe, maintain, and work modes.
Each cycle reads status, handles stale views, selects inner or outer loop action, records evidence, and stops on budget/risk/ambiguity.
Budgets include max cycles, wall time, writes, subagents, and risk level.
Push/version/archive behavior is gated by policy and checks.
non_goals: Run unbounded autonomous coding.
Bypass user approval for risky or destructive work.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test

evidence.summary: Added bounded codewiki_heartbeat tool contract and initial planner implementation for observe, maintain, and work modes with budgets, dry-run default, stop conditions, policy gates, and smoke coverage. Updated v2 operating model, product story, and task skill heartbeat policy.

TASK-023.title: ThinkCode query executor integration
TASK-023.summary: Use ThinkCode for ad hoc programmatic context creation, view validation, graph checks, and staged edits without making it CodeWiki's memory layer.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki can delegate bounded query/ETL and validation tasks to ThinkCode while preserving CodeWiki semantic write boundaries.
acceptance: ThinkCode role is documented in skills and runtime guidance.
At least one workflow uses ThinkCode for compact context creation or graph/view validation when available.
Fallback path works when ThinkCode is unavailable.
ThinkCode writes remain staged or flow through CodeWiki capabilities/transactions.
non_goals: Make ThinkCode required for CodeWiki.
Let ThinkCode edit generated views directly.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test

evidence.summary: Integrated optional ThinkCode context executor seam into heartbeat workflow: heartbeat now returns a bounded think_code_run script plan for view/task/graph context with native CodeWiki gateway/state fallback. Documented optional role and staged-write boundary in runtime/skills/README.

TASK-024.title: Sanitation checkpoints and git history policy
TASK-024.summary: Keep hot wiki state small by relying on git for full history and storing only compact semantic archives and release checkpoints.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki distinguishes hot current truth, generated views, compact semantic history, and full git recovery.
acceptance: Closed task archive stores compact semantic summaries with commit/release links rather than full raw context by default.
Release checkpoint schema records version, git sha, canonical digest, view schema version, and closed task summaries.
Docs and skills describe commit/push/version boundaries.
Views exclude cold history unless explicitly requested.
non_goals: Duplicate full git history inside `.wiki`.
Automatically push or version without policy gates.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test

evidence.summary: Updated roadmap closed-task retention to store compact semantic summaries instead of full raw context. Added checkpoint action to codewiki_task to snapshot release milestones with version labels, git shas, and canonical digests. Documented commit, push, and version boundaries in SKILL files. Verified behavior through new smoke tests.

TASK-025.title: Audit read model wording in README skills and tools
TASK-025.summary: Align public package language so generated read models are called views and source-of-truth files are called canonical truth.

goal.outcome: User-facing and agent-facing text use the same v2 vocabulary for views and canonical truth.
acceptance: README describes views and the canonical/view boundary.
Skills tell agents to consume views and avoid editing views.
Tool descriptions mention rebuilding views where relevant.
non_goals: Change storage paths.
Implement new view schemas.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test

evidence.summary: Audited and aligned read model wording across README, skills, and tools. Replaced "projection", "derived state", "generated state", and "read model" with "view", and "source of truth" with "canonical truth".

TASK-026.title: Decouple ThinkCode from CodeWiki memory policy
TASK-026.summary: Remove wording that frames ThinkCode as an enforced CodeWiki runtime and describe it only as optional compatible tooling.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki operates independently as a Pi package while allowing ThinkCode or other tools to integrate optionally.
acceptance: Skills do not require ThinkCode for CodeWiki workflows.
Code/task/verifier prompts frame programmatic context executors as optional/replaceable.
Knowledge and lexicon describe ThinkCode as compatible external tooling, not CodeWiki runtime.
Checks pass.
non_goals: Change ThinkCode package behavior.
Remove all mentions of ThinkCode where optional integration is useful.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test

evidence.summary: Removed ThinkCode-as-required-runtime wording from skills, prompts, and knowledge; now framed as optional external/compatible tooling governed by its own skill.

TASK-027.title: Simplify status panel tabs and header
TASK-027.summary: Update CodeWiki status panel UI to show repo-only header, Home/Product/System/Board tabs, deprecate Agents/Channels tabs, and render system Mermaid architecture with selectable components.

goal.outcome: Status panel presents user-focused Home, Product, System, and Board tabs while backend roadmap terminology remains unchanged.
acceptance: Header shows only the repo name.
Home shows overall traffic-light project status and meaningful factors influencing it.
Product shows users, stories, and surfaces entries only.
System renders the architecture Mermaid diagram and supports selectable components whose details open from component markdown.
Roadmap tab is renamed to Board in UI only; backend roadmap naming remains unchanged.
Agents and Channels tabs are removed or deprecated from the status panel UI.
non_goals: Rename backend roadmap/task concepts.
Implement external agent/channel systems.
verification: npm run typecheck -- --pretty false
npm test
manual/status panel smoke or existing rendering smoke coverage where available

evidence.summary: Simplified UI tabs to Home, Product, System, Board. Header simplified to repo name. Implemented traffic light status on Home tab. Product tab filters to core docs. System tab shows architecture components. Deprecated Agents and Channels tabs.

TASK-28.title: Stabilize CodeWiki refactor after partial DDD migration
TASK-28.summary: Restore a stable baseline after the partial folder refactor by mapping current drift, fixing broken CodeWiki tool execution, and removing duplicate or dead migration artifacts only when safe.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki has a green baseline before further architecture migration proceeds.
acceptance: `npm run typecheck` and `npm test` pass.
`codewiki_state` works from the Pi tool path or the failure is captured with a blocking root cause.
Current duplicate module sets are inventoried with a keep/delete/move decision.
No broad folder moves happen before the baseline is verified.
non_goals: Do not implement MCP, Codex, or Claude adapters.
Do not complete the full DDD migration in this task.
Do not delete compatibility shims without proving all imports moved.
verification: Run `npm run typecheck`.
Run `npm test`.
Run a CodeWiki state read or targeted equivalent for this repo.
Inspect git diff for unrelated rewrites.

TASK-29.title: Add architecture boundary checks and characterization coverage
TASK-29.summary: Add guardrails that freeze current behavior and prevent future domain/application/infrastructure/adapters import-boundary regressions during the refactor.

goal.outcome: Refactor work is protected by automated import-boundary checks and behavior characterization tests.
acceptance: A script checks that domain does not import Pi, Node I/O, application, infrastructure, adapters, or legacy core.
Application and infrastructure boundary rules are encoded with transitional exceptions where needed.
Smoke/characterization coverage exercises bootstrap, state read, task mutation, session mutation, and rebuild behavior at the current public surface.
Package scripts expose the checks so future agents can run one command before and after migration slices.
non_goals: Do not enforce final no-legacy-import rules until migration tasks remove shims.
Do not rewrite production architecture as part of adding checks.
verification: Run `npm run typecheck`.
Run `npm test`.
Run the new architecture check script.
Verify the check fails on an intentional forbidden import in local reasoning or unit fixture if practical.

TASK-30.title: Extract agent-agnostic application use cases behind ports
TASK-30.summary: Refactor CodeWiki behavior behind application use cases and ports while preserving Pi behavior through compatibility shims.

goal.outcome: Core CodeWiki workflows are callable without Pi-specific types through stable application-layer use cases.
acceptance: Application layer exposes use cases for read state, mutate task, manage session, run heartbeat, setup/bootstrap, and rebuild where appropriate.
Ports cover filesystem, project resolution, notification, session access, locking, message delivery, git/subprocess/rebuild needs without importing Pi types.
Domain/application modules do not import `@mariozechner/*` or Pi UI types.
Existing Pi tools and commands continue to work through adapters or compatibility wrappers.
non_goals: Do not move UI rendering internals until application seams are stable.
Do not add MCP/Codex/Claude adapters in this task.
verification: Run `npm run typecheck`.
Run `npm test`.
Run architecture boundary check.
Exercise Pi tool wrappers through smoke coverage or targeted tests.

TASK-31.title: Move Pi surface into adapters and retire legacy core structure
TASK-31.summary: Complete the folder migration only after guardrails and application seams exist, moving Pi commands/tools/UI into adapters and deleting legacy duplicates/shims.

goal.outcome: Repository layout matches the target ports/adapters architecture without duplicate engine/core surfaces.
acceptance: `extensions/codewiki/index.ts` is a thin Pi extension entrypoint that delegates registration to `src/adapters/pi`.
Pi tools, commands, and TUI code live under `src/adapters/pi`.
There is one engine/rebuild implementation path, with old duplicate `src/engine` removed or converted to a temporary shim before deletion.
`contracts.ts` exports stable public contracts from canonical locations, not legacy `core` internals.
Legacy `core`, `tools`, `commands`, `ui`, and duplicate `engine` shims are deleted only after import-boundary checks deny their use.
non_goals: Do not change public command/tool names.
Do not add non-Pi adapters beyond a tiny proof adapter if separately tasked.
verification: Run `npm run typecheck`.
Run `npm test`.
Run architecture boundary check with final no-legacy rules enabled.
Inspect package `files` and Pi extension discovery still include correct resources.

TASK-32.title: Prove the adapter seam with a minimal CLI surface
TASK-32.summary: After Pi migration is stable, add a minimal CLI adapter that calls the same application use cases to prove the agent-agnostic seam before larger MCP/Codex/Claude integrations.

goal.outcome: A second simple adapter validates that CodeWiki workflows are no longer Pi-bound.
acceptance: CLI exposes a minimal state read command and one task operation using application use cases.
CLI code does not duplicate domain/task/session logic from Pi tools.
Tests prove Pi and CLI paths share application behavior.
Findings from the CLI proof update future MCP/Codex/Claude adapter tasks if needed.
non_goals: Do not build a full CLI product.
Do not implement MCP server, Codex extension, or Claude Code integration in this task.
verification: Run `npm run typecheck`.
Run `npm test`.
Run architecture boundary check.
Run minimal CLI command against a temp fixture if packaged.

TASK-033.title: Checkpoint stable refactor baseline before next roadmap
TASK-033.summary: Verify and close the current refactor stabilization queue only after the hardened verification gateway is in place, then record a stable dogfooding baseline.

goal.outcome: After TASK-040 hardens the verifier, current TASK-28 through TASK-32 are verified, closed, and recorded as a stable dogfooding baseline.
acceptance: TASK-040 is done and `task-close` verification gateway is active.
TASK-28 through TASK-32 have fresh verification evidence from the hardened verifier and are either closed or explicitly blocked.
`npm test` passes on the stable baseline.
A release checkpoint records git SHA, canonical digest, view schema version, and closed-task summaries.
non_goals: Do not implement new sprint, skill, UI, heartbeat, or runtime features in this gate.
Do not rely on the previous weak verifier path for baseline closure.
verification: Run `npm test`.
Run `npm run typecheck` if available/required for the touched code.
Run `codewiki_state refresh=true` and confirm green health.

TASK-034.title: Design CodeWiki skill package and workflow assets
TASK-034.summary: Define a CodeWiki skill package with router instructions, deterministic scripts, and grill/plan/build/verify workflow assets adapted from Matt Pocock's skills only where they fit CodeWiki semantics.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki has a clear packaged skill contract that teaches agents how to operate `.wiki` without creating parallel documentation systems.
acceptance: A skill-package structure is specified for `SKILL.md`, `scripts/`, and workflow assets.
`grill`, `plan`, `build`, and `verify` loops are described with CodeWiki-specific inputs, outputs, stop conditions, and canonical write rules.
Matt Pocock skill ideas are classified as adopt, adapt, or discard, with documentation-related conventions explicitly filtered through CodeWiki's knowledge model.
Repo-specific process customization lives under `.wiki/knowledge/**` or config, while package workflow assets remain versioned and not casually self-mutated.
non_goals: Do not blindly copy `CONTEXT.md`, `docs/adr`, issue-tracker, or PRD conventions when they duplicate CodeWiki knowledge/roadmap semantics.
Do not make agents hand-edit generated views.
verification: Review packaged skill docs against `.wiki` invariants.
Run package smoke/test checks after any skill packaging changes.
Verify no parallel source of truth was introduced.

TASK-035.title: Enforce structured knowledge-roadmap-code mapping
TASK-035.summary: Strengthen CodeWiki schemas, lint, and docs so knowledge docs, sprints, tasks, evidence, and code paths form a navigable vertical mapping for agents.

goal.outcome: Agents can reliably trace intended knowledge to roadmap delta to code ownership through enforced metadata and generated views.
acceptance: Knowledge frontmatter requirements are documented and enforced for ids, summaries, owners, and relevant code/spec links.
Roadmap tasks validate `spec_paths`, `code_paths`, goals, acceptance, non-goals, verification, and evidence links.
Lint/reporting identifies missing or broken horizontal and vertical mappings.
Generated views expose compact navigation packets for this mapping without becoming canonical truth.
non_goals: Do not require every code file to have a one-to-one doc.
Do not make roadmap tasks duplicate all knowledge content.
verification: Run `npm test`.
Run a rebuild and confirm `codewiki_state refresh=true` is green.
Add/adjust smoke fixtures for invalid mapping cases.

TASK-036.title: Introduce sprint planning model
TASK-036.summary: Add a roadmap sprint layer so the roadmap holds broad intent, future sprints stay outcome-level, and only the next sprint is decomposed into executable tasks.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki supports `roadmap -> sprints -> tasks` while keeping task execution atomic and verifiable.
acceptance: Sprint semantics are documented: future sprints contain outcomes/spec links/risks, next sprint contains tasks.
Roadmap schema and generated views can represent sprint summaries and next-sprint tasks.
Planning workflow creates tasks only for the next sprint.
Sprint closure requires integration verification and drift review.
non_goals: Do not build a full project-management suite.
Do not force existing flat roadmap users through a breaking migration without compatibility.
verification: Run schema/view smoke tests.
Verify old flat roadmap fixtures remain readable or have a safe migration path.
Run `codewiki_state refresh=true` and inspect queue/status views.

TASK-037.title: Build standalone read-only CodeWiki UI
TASK-037.summary: Detach the human status interface from Pi by creating an agent-agnostic read-only UI that consumes generated `.wiki/views/**` and renders Mermaid outside the Pi TUI constraints.

goal.outcome: Humans can inspect CodeWiki status, roadmap, product/system views, and Mermaid architecture without running Pi.
acceptance: A standalone read-only UI surface is designed or implemented without importing Pi SDK/TUI code.
UI consumes generated views as its only data source for initial slice.
Mermaid architecture renders in an environment suited to diagrams.
Pi status panel remains compatible or delegates shared formatting where useful.
non_goals: Do not add canonical writes in the first external UI slice.
Do not make UI the access layer for agents; agents should use views, CLI, MCP, or native adapters.
verification: Run package tests.
Manually open/read fixture views in the standalone UI or snapshot-render output.
Confirm no Pi imports cross into standalone UI modules.

TASK-038.title: Implement trigger-based heartbeat agency loop
TASK-038.summary: Replace the current heartbeat planning stub with a bounded trigger-based loop whose names match user-facing moments such as task end, sprint end, roadmap end, and budget end.

goal.outcome: Users can choose understandable heartbeat triggers and safe autonomy budgets instead of opaque agency levels.
acceptance: Heartbeat trigger names align with user-facing events: task-end, sprint-end, roadmap-end, and budget-end.
Each trigger maps to bounded observe/maintain/plan/work behavior with explicit budgets and stop conditions.
Cron-like operation is limited to read-only or low-risk drift/audit maintenance unless explicit policy permits more.
Heartbeat can enter a brainstorm/planning loop only when trigger, budget, and risk policy allow it.
non_goals: Do not implement unbounded autonomous coding.
Do not push, release, archive, or mutate high-risk state without explicit approval and green checks.
verification: Add tests or smoke coverage for trigger-to-action planning.
Run `npm test`.
Dry-run heartbeat scenarios and inspect returned stop reasons/actions.

TASK-039.title: Provide optional ThinkCode runtime adapter
TASK-039.summary: Make ThinkCode a first-class optional runtime for bounded CodeWiki context work while preserving native fallback paths for Pi, Codex, Claude Code, and other agent harnesses.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki can prefer ThinkCode when available but remains usable from any agent runtime through views, CLI/gateway, MCP/future adapters, or native tools.
acceptance: Runtime docs describe ThinkCode as optional, not required memory or execution substrate.
Adapter contract identifies where `think-code.policy.json` lives and how staged writes are validated.
Fallback paths are documented for native CodeWiki gateway/views when ThinkCode is unavailable.
The design supports future Codex and Claude Code adapters without Pi-specific assumptions.
non_goals: Do not make ThinkCode a hard dependency.
Do not let ThinkCode bypass CodeWiki canonical write rules or mutate generated views directly.
verification: Run tests/smoke after docs/package changes.
Validate gateway manifest still advertises CodeWiki capabilities independent of ThinkCode.
Check skills mention optional fallback behavior clearly.

TASK-040.title: Harden verification gateway before dogfood checkpoint
TASK-040.summary: Implement a first-class CodeWiki verification gateway/router before relying on existing verifier evidence to close the stable refactor baseline and start dogfooding.

goal.outcome: Task closure and future sprint/release/drift checks use a robust verification gateway instead of ad hoc verifier prompts or parent-session confidence, using hardened context packs from the view/context-compiler work where needed.
acceptance: Define a `codewiki.verify`-style internal gateway/router contract with profiles, briefs, policies, and verdict schema.
Implement the `task-close` profile first and route `codewiki_task action="close"` through it.
Run deterministic preflight before semantic verification, including task goal fields, linked path existence, evidence/check presence, and view freshness where applicable.
Consume or define a compact verifier context pack instead of relying on ad hoc task prompt assembly.
Invoke semantic verification in a separate fresh read-only process/session/subagent with a complete CodeWiki verifier rubric and compact task brief.
Parse and validate verifier JSON strictly; malformed output returns `block` with evidence.
Add golden or smoke coverage for `pass`, `fail`, `block`, and malformed-output cases.
Ensure verifier cannot mutate canonical truth or generated views; parent remains sole writer for evidence and lifecycle changes.
non_goals: Do not implement all profiles in the first slice beyond contract stubs or documented routing.
Do not make verification depend on ThinkCode or any single runtime.
Do not close baseline refactor tasks using the old weak verifier path.
Do not solve the full sprint/roadmap context compiler in this task.
verification: Run `npm test`.
Run `npm run typecheck` if required by touched TypeScript.
Manually exercise or unit-test `task-close` verifier outcomes for pass/fail/block.
Run `codewiki_state refresh=true` and confirm green health.

TASK-041.title: Review and improve generated views into a token-efficient context compiler
TASK-041.summary: Audit and redesign generated `.wiki/views/**` as lightweight routing and observability read models, then benchmark prebuilt packs versus bounded programmatic exploration to choose the most token-efficient context strategy for task/sprint/roadmap work.

goal.outcome: Generated views become a first-class context routing layer that supports compact role-specific packs, graph slices, and optional bounded programmatic exploration without confusing observability with verification gates.
acceptance: Document the context-access model: views provide map/cached routes, bounded sandbox/programmatic tools provide microscope-style exploration, and verifier gateways remain the only quality gate.
Benchmark or prototype four context strategies for representative task work: prebuilt pack only, progressive disclosure only, sandbox/programmatic exploration only, and hybrid pack plus sandbox.
Define role-specific seed/context pack contracts for at least `grill`, `plan`, `build`, and `verify`, emphasizing minimal routing metadata instead of large precomputed context dumps.
Define typed graph-slice views for task/sprint/roadmap scopes with `core`, `supporting`, `watch`, and `excluded` tiers plus edge reasons and confidence (`explicit` vs `inferred`).

Define horizontal and vertical observability signals only: knowledge/task/evidence/view alignment and intent → knowledge → roadmap → code → checks/evidence alignment; do not make observability a pass/fail gate.
Define an acceptance/evidence matrix that maps criteria to spec paths, code paths, checks, and evidence for verifier consumption.
Represent token budget as generator/test metadata (`estimated_tokens`, `max_tokens`, `over_budget`) rather than instructions the agent must maintain manually.
Implement or prototype the smallest useful `task.build`, `task.verify`, and `task.graph` views needed by the hardened verification gateway, without blocking future sprint/roadmap expansion.
Add tests or smoke checks proving generated context views stay deterministic, stale-aware, and within size guarantees.
non_goals: Do not introduce embeddings, vector search, or external search infrastructure in the first slice.
Do not build the standalone UI; only shape views so UI and agents can consume them later.
Do not replace canonical knowledge, roadmap, evidence, or verifier verdicts with generated views.
Do not make observability itself a closure gate; verification consumes observability but decides separately.
Do not hand-edit generated view files.
verification: Run view rebuild and confirm `.wiki/views/status.json` or task contexts point to the new context entrypoints where appropriate.
Run `npm test`.
Run `npm run typecheck` if TypeScript files change.
Run `codewiki_state refresh=true` and confirm health stays green.
Manually inspect one generated task seed/graph pack for token efficiency, recommended next reads, and acceptance/evidence mapping.
